title: FOODEEG: An open dataset of human electroencephalographic and behavioural responses to food images

affiliations.name: Division of Science, New York University Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
Melbourne School of Psychological Sciences, University of Melbourne, Australia

description: FOODEEG is an open dataset comprising electroencephalographic (EEG) and behavioral responses from 117 participants to food images across two testing sessions. The dataset includes raw and processed EEG recordings during a food categorization task, behavioral responses from food go/no-go and paired choice tasks, participant questionnaires assessing dietary styles and motivational tendencies, and normative ratings of food stimuli from 624 online participants across 22 food attributes. This resource enables investigation of neural and behavioral mechanisms underlying food perception and decision-making.
methods_description: EEG and behavioral data were collected over two sessions. Session 1 involved a food categorization task where participants viewed food images for 2 seconds and categorized them (yes/no) on healthiness, tastiness, or willingness to eat, followed by continuous 0-100 ratings. Session 2 included a food go/no-go task and a food paired choice task. Normative ratings were collected from online samples on 22 food attributes including nutritive, hedonic, taste, familiarity, and emotional properties.
